params.focusReason: control-frame calibration; LM focused to read her per-hit damage (neutral Water boss, all 4 units neutral, x1.10 element factored separately)

method: ffmpeg contact sheets (overview 1/6s; FB-window zoom fps=2-3; full frames @44-51s). Read from stills with the owner-confirmed colour convention.
notes: DoT-crit test — INCONCLUSIVE (attribution corrected by scripts/probe/hit-values.ts). Colour convention (owner-confirmed): white=normal, orange+crit-icon=crit, red 'CORE HIT'=core, bright GREEN=HEAL (green here = Helm's B3 life-leech team heal, over the CHARACTER not the crosshair — NOT LM damage, discarded). CORRECTION: LM's value bands per hit-values are SMG normal 14k-68k, DoT (253.4% modelled; in-game 63.36% x4 = ~156k-220k per sub-hit), barrage/big ~2.25M+. So the 64733 popups I first tagged 'dot' are actually her NORMAL band, and 82714 (orange) is a NORMAL crit (normals always crit — not the test). The real DoT sub-hits (~156-220k) sit in a CLEAN band (above ≤68k normals, below ≥2.25M barrage) but were not isolated in this read. 450314 (orange) is too large for a DoT crit (likely a normal core-crit or barrage). Net: LM DoT crit NOT confirmed here; ginmy dot_test remains the empirical basis. Re-read needed targeting the ~156-220k band. Cyan numbers = boss hits on the focus unit's Crown shield (not LM damage). RE-READ 2026-07-15 (DoT sub-hit band, FB window @44.9-54.9s; frames in scratchpad full48/tick49/tickZ): FB windows located via the top-centre 10.00->0 Full-Burst COUNTDOWN timer (decisive: e.g. 06.60->05.92 across t=48.4-49.1). SINGLE boss confirmed (one central mech + one HP bar; surrounding shapes are flying debris/parts, not separate enemies). DoT sub-hit value MEASURED = 337,736 (white, non-crit), band ABOVE normals (64,733) and below barrage. (1) FB ON DoT = YES: control-comp sim steady-buff FB tick (LM focus, all 4 units) = 1,321,868 for the full 253.44% => one 63.36% sub-hit = 330,467 WITH FB (major 1.5); WITHOUT FB (major 1.0) it would be 220,311. Measured 337,736 matches the FB-boosted 330,467 to 2.2% and is nowhere near the base 220,311 (would need implausible >3x buff). Sim already applies FB correctly to the DoT (DBG: major=1.500 in-FB, 1.000 for the 1-2 ticks that land just after FB-end). (2) SUB-HITS/SEC on the single boss = 4 (all pellets land): with one enemy, '63.36% x4 to random enemies' has only the boss to target; DoT-band popups (white 337,736 + occasional orange crit) stack on the boss and per tick I resolve up to ~4 (e.g. the t=48.3 tick showed 1 orange 450,314 crit + ~3 white 337,736 = 4). Exact frame-count is obscured by overlap with the dense ~20/s SMG normal (64,733) stream, so 'exactly 4' is MEDIUM-HIGH not certain, but the count never EXCEEDS 4 and each rendered sub-hit is a full-magnitude 63.36%xFB hit. (3) NEW: DoT sub-hits DO CRIT in-game (measured 450,314 orange = 337,736 x1.333 FB-crit factor; this is the prior read's mystery '450314' — it was a DoT crit, NOT a barrage). BUT the sim's DoT is modelled WITHOUT crit (DOT_CRIT defaults OFF, sim.ts:42; LM not in XCRIT; DBG DoT major=1.500 flat) => sim UNDER-models the DoT by the ~7.5% crit expectation. (4) CONTEXT: sim over-predicts LM total by 22% (sim 517.4M vs real 423.1M from lm.jpg) while teammates match well (Crown 0.98, Helm 0.96, Snow White 1.04). Sim buckets: normal 282.4M, DoT 155.8M, barrage 79.1M. The overshoot is NOT the DoT count: if <4 sub-hits landed AND the DoT crits (measured), reconciling the total needs ~1.5 effective sub-hits, contradicting the clear visual of multiple full sub-hits — so LM's overshoot lives elsewhere (normals: ACR/core rate/cadence), not in the DoT sub-hit count.

popups.note: ORANGE crit — but of a NORMAL (64733 x1.278), not the DoT
white; upper normal band / small overlap
ORANGE crit; RE-READ: this is a DoT sub-hit CRIT (337736 x1.333 FB-crit), not a barrage
RE-READ 2026-07-15: WHITE DoT sub-hit (63.36% x FB), FB window (countdown ~06.5). = sim FB-boosted 330467 (tick/4); base non-FB would be 220311. FB confirmed on DoT.
RE-READ: ORANGE DoT sub-hit CRIT = 337736 x1.333 (FB crit factor). DoT DOES crit in-game; sim models DoT without crit (DOT_CRIT off).
RE-READ: RED 'CORE HIT' — a NORMAL core hit (not DoT; DoT/skill never core). Recorded for completeness.
